message.content: Heydon here with my full accessibility review of the Waiting For implementation. ## BLOCKING Issues ### 1. Accessible names on buttons lack `waiting_on` disambiguation In `templates/waiting_for_item.html`, the aria-labels are: - `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }}"` - `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }}"` - `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }}"` As I flagged in my pre-implementation review: if a user has two items with the same title but different `waiting_on` values (e.g., "Invoice" waiting on Alice vs. "Invoice" waiting on Bob), these buttons are indistinguishable to screen reader users. **Fix**: Include `waiting_on` in the aria-labels: - `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` Same fix needed in `waiting_for_edit.html` for the Cancel link: - `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` This is a WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) issue. Buttons that perform different actions on different items MUST be distinguishable. ### 2. Date display lacks `` element In `templates/waiting_for_item.html` line 6: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` This should use the `` element with a machine-readable `datetime` attribute: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` This requires adding a `date_added_iso` field to `WaitingForView` (ISO 8601 format like `2026-02-12`) alongside the human-friendly `date_added`. The `` element is semantic HTML — it helps assistive technology and search engines parse dates correctly. This is not ARIA, it's native HTML semantics, so it's the right tool. I know no other template in the project uses `` yet, but this feature explicitly shows dates as a core part of each item's identity ("date added" is one of three required display fields per US-GTD-5). We should get it right here and set the pattern. ## Minor Issues ### 3. Edit form labels are visually hidden — consider consistency In `templates/waiting_for_edit.html`, the labels are `class="visually-hidden"`: ```html ``` This is technically accessible (screen readers will read the labels), but it differs from the add form which has visible labels. The existing edit patterns in next_action_edit and project_edit also use visually-hidden labels, so this is consistent with the codebase. Not blocking — just noting it for the record. ### 4. `waiting_on` display lacks semantic distinction for screen readers In the item partial, the `waiting_on` person is just a ``: ```html {{ item.waiting_on }} ``` A screen reader user hearing "Invoice Finance team" won't know that "Finance team" is the person/entity being waited on. Consider adding a visually hidden prefix: ```html Waiting on: {{ item.waiting_on }} ``` This gives screen reader users the context they need to parse the item structure. Minor because the page heading "Waiting For" provides some context, and the `waiting_on` appears in the aria-labels once the first BLOCKING issue is fixed. ## Nice-to-have ### 5. Cancel in edit form is `` not `
